text:
SUMMARY

This is AI generated summarization, which may have errors. For context, always refer to the full article.

MANILA, Philippines –Nesthy Peteciolooks to stay in the hunt for an Olympic boxing gold that has eluded the country as she leads four Filipinos competing in the Paris Games on Thursday, August 8.

Here is Team Philippines’ schedule (Manila time):

From joint 13th, Bianca Pagdanganan boosts her medal bid as she ends the first two rounds of the Paris Olympics women’s golf competition at joint 6th with a 3-under 141 aggregate.

Dottie Ardina, meanwhile, climbs four spots to joint 36th with 148.

Read the full storyhere.

Elreen Ando finishes sixth in the women’s 59kg division as she gets the job done in the clean and jerk, lifting 130kg on her third and final attempt.

The Cebuana ends her second Olympic stint without a medal, but she resets her personal best in clean and jerk by 2kg.

Ando totals 230kg, also a personal best.

China’s Luo Shifang claims the gold with an Olympic record of 241kg total, while Canada’s Maude Charron (236kg) and Chinese Taipei’s Kuo Hsing-Chun (235kg) bag silver and bronze, respectively.

Elreen Ando incurs a no lift to start the clean and jerk as she fails to clear 130kg.

Elreen Ando ends the snatch round at joint seventh place with 100kg as she records a no lift at 102kg on her third and final attempt.

She set a starting weight of 130kg in the clear and jerk phase.

China’s Luo Shifang sets a new Olympic record in snatch with 107kg.

Elreen Ando gets herself on the board after clearing 100kg on her second attempt in the snatch portion.

Nesthy Petecio concludes her Olympic campaign with a bronze after a split decision loss to Poland’s Julia Szeremeta in the women’s 57kg semifinals.

Petecio sweeps the judges’ scorecards in the opening round, but Szeremeta fights back and wins by scores of 29-28, 29-28, 29-28, 29-28, 28-29.

An Olympic boxing gold remains elusive for the Philippines as all of its five boxers bow out.

Eyeing a return trip to the final of the women’s 57kg division, Petecio – the only remaining Filipino boxer in contention – battles Poland’s Julia Szeremeta.

A win over Szeremeta will guarantee Petecio a second straight Olympic silver.

Meanwhile, golfersBianca Pagdanganan and Dottie Ardinaresume their campaigns in the women’s golf competition as they seek to climb the standings in the second round.

Pagdanganan and Ardina ended the first of four rounds at joint 13th and joint 40th place, respectively.

Later in the day, weightlifterElreen Andoshoots for a medal in her second Olympic stint as she sees action in the women’s 59kg class.

The Philippines is assured of four medals in Paris, with gymnastCarlos Yulodelivering a pair of golds, boxerAira Villegasbagging bronze, and Petecio hoping to add another mint.– Rappler.com

title: HIGHLIGHTS, RESULTS: Team Philippines, 2024 Paris Olympics – August 8
author: delfin.dioquino editor
description: The only remaining Filipino boxer in contention, Nesthy Petecio eyes a return trip to the final as she leads four Filipinos competing in the Paris Olympics on Thursday, August 8
